trajectory_summary: Clean, efficient call that reaches a booked appointment in under nine minutes with a cooperative customer. Carter completes most verify items, holds setter scope on all three closer-tier questions, and books without friction. The call's weakness is not what went wrong but what was never built: the bill was captured and dropped without an emotional flip, the EV enthusiasm signal was acknowledged and abandoned, the wife on title was noted but not included in the appointment, and the in-person vs. phone appointment type conflict was never resolved. The appointment exists but carries structural fragility.
outcome_dispute: The call ends with a booked appointment for 12PM the following day. However, the appointment type is ambiguous: Carter confirmed an in-person visit at 03:00 ('We do come in person. Yeah.') while the codex button_up protocol specifies a phone appointment with a closer. If Ion's standard closer appointment is by phone, Carter may have booked the wrong appointment type. Additionally, the wife on title was identified at 01:38 but was not included in the appointment — a protocol violation that makes this a structurally conditional booking. The outcome tag should reflect 'booked but fragile' rather than a clean sit.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: Are mornings or afternoons or evenings typically best for you for an appointment?
primary_coaching_focus.why: Carter books the appointment but leaves four of six required button_up elements on the table — no one-hour block, no no-driving/computer anchor, no 'is that fair' handshake, and no spouse inclusion despite the wife being on title. The no-driving/computer anchor and the verbal handshake are the two elements most directly tied to show rate. Combined with the unresolved in-person vs. phone appointment type and the excluded spouse, this booking carries more fragility than the cooperative customer tone suggests. This is the highest-leverage coaching gap on this call because it affects whether Carter gets paid.

key_moments.quote: To offset the the bill and, charge electric, vehicle.
key_moments.stewart_read: Zoltan volunteers two distinct motivations in one breath: bill reduction AND an EV. The EV is a load-amplifier — it materially increases the value of the solar system and the urgency of the decision. Carter acknowledges with 'Okay. Cool.' and moves directly to the townhouse question. The EV signal is never returned to. This is the call's highest-value uncaptured moment.

Carter correctly captures the wife on title — the verify item is checked. But the protocol requires that the spouse be included in the appointment, not just noted. Carter moves immediately to roof age without asking whether the wife can be on the 12PM call. The appointment is booked at 07:28 with no mention of the wife. This is a protocol violation: the verify item was completed but the downstream action it requires was not taken.

key_moments.quote: Can we do this online or somebody has to come here?
key_moments.stewart_read: Zoltan explicitly asks whether the appointment can be done remotely before accepting in-person. Carter confirms in-person at 03:00 ('We do come in person. Yeah.') and Zoltan accepts ('No problem.'). However, the codex button_up protocol specifies a phone appointment with a closer — not an in-person visit. This creates two compounding risks: (1) if Ion's standard closer appointment is by phone, Carter booked the wrong appointment type; (2) even if in-person is correct for this market, Zoltan's preference signal ('Can we do this online') means he accepted a channel he didn't prefer. Accepted-but-not-preferred channels elevate no-show risk.

key_moments.quote: Cool. Do you have a second to send that right now so I can check it out before we get off the phone?
key_moments.stewart_read: Carter successfully collects the bill — the PDF arrives via email at approximately 06:49 and Carter confirms receipt at 07:03. The bill collection itself is completed. The gap is what happens after: Carter immediately pivots to scheduling ('Are mornings or afternoons or evenings typically best for you?') without any emotional engagement with the bill's content. The bill was captured as data and dropped. No flip, no acknowledgment of what the numbers mean, no alignment-against-utility. Zoltan had already volunteered his motivation (bill offset + EV) — the bill was the moment to connect those dots.
